name: Caramelized Banana Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 3 large eggs - room temp, slightly beaten
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F.
2. Place pecans on a baking sheet; toast in the oven for about 8 minutes, checking frequently to avoid burning. Set aside.
3. Melt 1 tbsp of butter in a non-stick skillet over medium heat. Stir in brown sugar and cook until melted (about 6 minutes). Add 2 thinly sliced bananas and cook until caramelized and soft enough to mash with the back of a spoon. Remove from heat and set aside.
4. In a large bowl, combine cream cheese and 1 cup of sugar. Beat until completely blended.
5. Add sour cream, eggs (room temperature, slightly beaten), vanilla extract, and heavy cream to the mixture. Stir until incorporated.
6. Combine the remaining tablespoon of butter, remaining 1/2 cup of sugar, and toasted pecans in a food processor fitted with a metal blade. Process until finely ground.
7. Press the mixture onto the bottom of an ungreased 8-inch springform pan. Pack firmly using the palm of your hand.
8. Pour the filling mixture over the crust.
9. Place the pan inside a larger pan and put it on the oven rack. Add water to a depth of about 2 inches.
10. Bake until firm to touch, about 1-1/2 hours.
11. Remove from the oven and let cool on a wire rack for at least 1 hour. Refrigerate, covered, for at least 3 hours or overnight before serving.
